input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` doesn't work in .NET 10 (it was deprecated/removed). Let me provide guidance based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI changes.

In .NET 9+, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated on Android because the underlying Android API (`SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`) was deprecated in Android 11 (API 30). 

The modern approach in .NET MAUI (.NET 9+) is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior, which is enabled by default. But for more control, especially for chat-like UIs, the recommended approach is to use Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` / edge-to-edge APIs.


For .NET 10, the real solution for a chat UI is to handle window insets properly by configuring the window to not automatically fit system windows, then managing the layout padding based on keyboard visibility. The `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior handles basic scrolling, but for a full layout resize effect you need to work with Android's inset system directly. In .NET 10, the older `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` approach was removed, so I need to use `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` on Android to detect keyboard insets and adjust padding, while iOS can rely on `UIKeyboard` notifications or MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`. For a chat UI specifically, I can listen to keyboard changes and dynamically adjust the page's bottom padding to shift the entire layout up.
